entry.title: Re: Foundational Elements for Harnessing the Power of Business AI in Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public E
entry.content: Thanks for this article! Was looking for some more structure in all the AI solutions SAP is releasing to the market, this made it a lot clearer. Will definitely check out the various links to articles and examples! Now to build our own implementation and get everything to work together 🙂

entry.title: Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping for nonrecurring pay components
entry.content:
Hello,

I am seeking recommendations and best practices on the following issue. We are seeing nonrecurring pay components with a value of $0 coming over from the RCM Offer Approval into Onboarding as they are mapped in Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping. The business would have to manually delete the $0 pay components in Onboarding before submitting.

RCM Offer Approval:

Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping:

Onboarding:

Is there a way to prevent $0 nonrecurring pay components from coming over into Onboarding so we can prevent the manual work involved in deleting those pay components before submitting?

The behavior I’m observing in the system is all-or-nothing: when they are mapped, everything, including the $0 pay components, is transferred over. If they are not mapped, then the business would have to manually add those pay components.

entry.title: SAP HR Integration using MuleSoft
entry.content:
SAP HR systems is the main source of data for Employees starting from their recruitment till they are Terminated/retired from the company and even beyond that.

So, we would need HR data across various platforms – SAP & Non-SAP for various uses. These integrations help organizations achieve a seamless HR ecosystem, improve data accuracy, and enhance overall efficiency.

Some of the common examples are outlined below:

  • Payroll Systems (Eg: ADP/Workbrain)
  • Talent Management Systems (Eg: Workday/Cornerstone
  • Learning Management Systems (Eg; - Moodle/Talent LMS)
  • Benefits Administration Systems (Eg: bswift/BenefitFocus)
  • Time and Attendance Systems (Eg: Kronos/ADP WorkForce)
  • Employee Self-Service Portals (Eg: Service Now)
  • Third-Party HR Analytics Tools (Eg: Visier/Workday Analytics)

In this blog, I will talk about the cloud-based integration technique using MuleSoft which can be used for integrations from SAP HR. We can have a Remote Function call designed using ABAP and the same can be re-used to generate various interface files as per the vendor requirement.

Creating reusable RFC (Remote Function Call) function modules in SAP has several benefits, especially when integrating with platforms like MuleSoft. Below are some of the key advantages:

  • Reduced Development Time
  • Consistency
  • Simplified Maintenance
  • Easier Testing
  • Flexible Integrations
  • Quick Deployment
  • Lower Development Costs
  • Reduced Training Costs
  • Optimized Code
  • Reduced Redundancy
  • Comprehensive Documentation
  • Standardized Interfaces
  • Centralized Control
  • Compliance and Security

MuleSoft is a powerful platform for integration and API management, enabling organizations to connect applications, data, and devices. It's particularly useful for creating seamless integrations with SAP ECC HR systems.

Below are the detailed steps to set up MuleSoft

Step 1: Set Up Your Environment

  • Install MuleSoft: Download and install MuleSoft Anypoint Platform.
  • Install SAP Java Connector (JCo): Ensure you have the SAP Java Connector libraries installed, as MuleSoft uses JCo for RFC communication.

Step 2: Create RFC Destination in SAP

  • Open SAP ECC: Use transaction code SM59 to create an RFC destination.
  • Configure RFC Destination: Enter the connection details for MuleSoft, such as the host, port, and system ID.

Step 3: Develop RFC Function Modules in SAP (ABAP Developer can help create an RFC in SAP)

  • Create RFC Function Modules: Develop function modules in SAP ECC that will be called by MuleSoft. These modules should expose the necessary functionality (e.g., fetching employee data)
  • Test Function Modules: Ensure the function modules work correctly within SAP.

Step 4: Set Up MuleSoft Integration

  • Create a New Project: Open Anypoint Studio and create a new Mule project.
  • Add SAP Connector: Use the SAP connector in MuleSoft to connect to the SAP system. Configure the connector with the RFC destination details.
  • Develop Integration Flow: Create a flow in MuleSoft to call the RFC function modules. Use DataWeave for data transformation if needed.

Step 5: Test and Deploy

  • Test Integration: Test the integration flow to ensure it correctly calls the RFC function modules and processes the data.
  • Deploy Integration: Deploy the integration flow to a MuleSoft runtime environment.

Additional Resources

MuleSoft Documentation: Refer to the SAP Connector User Guide for detailed instructions and examples.

MuleSoft Community: Join the MuleSoft community forums for support and additional resources.

 

While MuleSoft is a powerful integration platform, there are some limitations to consider when using it for SAP HR integrations:

  • Steep Learning Curve
  • Customization Challenges
  • Initial Investment & Ongoing Maintenance
  • Dependence on Third-Party Connectors
  • Limited Out-of-the-Box Support

Despite these limitations, MuleSoft remains a powerful tool for integrating SAP HR systems with other applications and systems. Proper planning, expertise, and ongoing maintenance can help overcome these challenges and achieve successful integrations.

 

Conclusion:

Integrating SAP HR systems with Mulesoft offers a powerful solution to streamline and enhance human resource management processes. By leveraging Mulesoft's robust API capabilities, organizations can seamlessly connect SAP On Premise HCM & SuccessFactors Employee Central with other HR systems, ensuring real-time data synchronization, improved operational efficiency, and a unified view of employee information. This integration not only supports better decision-making but also enhances the overall employee experience, driving strategic growth and competitive advantage.

Happy Exploring & Learning!!

entry.title: Re: Add delay in Request message SAP PO
entry.content: I think you are looking to make use of the sync/async bridge for something like you are describing.

entry.title: Re: Statistical WBS in EKKN
entry.content: did you ever find an answer? i have similar problem but I am selecting account assignment as P while system is populating both cost center and project while we dont know where cost center is coming from

entry.title: Delay in publishing data from SAP system to 3rd party enterprise portal
entry.content:
Hi,

I have encountered an issue in publishing data from SAP ERP (ECC system) to Enterprise portal. there is a delay of 10 mins or so in receiving data in Enterprise portal through outbound IDoC from SAP ECC system. especially in receiving the pricing details for a product; pricing table (custom table) maintained in SAP ECC system.

both SAP system and Enterprise portal are connected through a synchronous RFC using O data services.

could someone reply

1. How to approach this problem?

2  what are the validation checks I need to perform?

do the needful please.

regards

Venu

entry.title: ILX Curriculum Status Bubble
entry.content:
Hello!

I am currently working through the Integrated Learning Experience (ILX) release notes. I understand that the curriculum status bubble changes from Curriculum Compliance Current to Curriculum Compliance Expired when a training is past due; however, I do not understand when the bubble changes from Curriculum Compliance Current to Curriculum Compliance Ending. I am assuming the status changes to Curriculum Compliance Ending when an assigned learning is within so many days of its due date, but I am not sure what that threshold is. 

For example, I currently have training assigned to me that is due 11/30/2025 and my bubble says Curriculum Compliance Current. At what point will my bubble change from Curriculum Compliance Current to Curriculum Compliance Ending?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: OData authorization Error in Pulic Cloud
entry.content:
Some months ago I faced a similar issue when deploying an app that used an API.

I found SAP Note "3424818 - Restricted usage of S/4 released API services as found on the SAP BusinessAccelerator Hub in custom UIs". This note mentions the following "It is not supported to use S/4 released API services as found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for custom UI applications. Using these API services for custom UIs is prevented during the deployment of the UI application to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system"

I'm a bit surprised you didn't get an error while you deployed the app. I thought that was not permitted.

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas - Admin question or rather your best pra...
entry.content:
You could use the "push to client" feature to send your objects from client 210 to 200 and then change their original system/client to 200 once they are there.

After that, you can use the regular transport system from client 200 to distribute your Personas objects across your landscape.

entry.title: Lean Warehouse Overview and Implementation Benefits
entry.content:
Hi

Today I am going to share this blog for Lean WM Implementation benefits.

Lean Warehouse Management in SAP

The lean warehouse solution focuses on simplicity and efficiency. It is tailored for businesses with less complex warehousing needs, often characterized by lower volumes and fewer product variations. Lean warehouses streamline operations by minimizing unnecessary processes and focusing on the core functionalities necessary for effective warehouse management. When you implement the Warehouse Management System (WMS), you manage goods movements and stock changes in the warehouse at storage bin level. However, if you implement Lean WM.  inventory management takes place solely at storage location level. The system does not update the stock data at storage bin level using the quants. You use Lean WM solely for processing goods receipts and goods issues. Using Lean WM, you process the warehouse movements in basically the same way as if using the Warehouse Management System: you work with deliveries, and you create transfer orders for these deliveries. These transfer orders serve as pick lists

Functionalities and Features

Lean warehouses offer a pared-down set of functionalities that cover essential warehouse operations, including:

- Basic inbound and outbound processing

- Simple inventory management

- Standard RF transactions

- Basic reporting and analytics

The use of transfer orders in Lean WM provides the following advantages:

You can reprint transfer orders at any time.

You can split transfer orders and thus distribute the workload better among the staff in your warehouse.

You can pass on the transfer order target data and the names of those who process transfer orders to the HR application component, where this data can be used to calculate incentive wage.

If you do no wish to update stocks in your fixed bin warehouse at storage bin level, Lean WM provides you with the option of using the advantages of warehouse management through transfer orders in the same way as warehouse management using the WMS. Since Lean WM does not update stocks at storage bin level, you can display the stock quantities solely in Inventory Management (IM) and not with the stock overview function in WM. Also, you cannot process possible stock differences that occur in the warehouse using Lean WM, only using Inventory Management (MM-IM)

 

 

Implementation and Maintenance

SAP recommends that you implement Lean WM if you wish to pick deliveries in a warehouse that you are not managing using WMS. Lean warehouse implementations are quicker and less resource-intensive. The streamlined nature of the solution means fewer customizations and simpler configuration processes. Maintenance is also more straightforward, with fewer components to manage and update, resulting in lower ongoing costs.

Prerequisites

You can only implement Lean WM in a fixed bin warehouse; random storage is not possible. Before you can implement Lean WM, you need to make the following configuration changes in Customizing:

  1. Assign a (newly defined) storage location to a warehouse number.
  2. Set up at least two storage types:
  • a picking storage type as the source storage type
  • A shipping area as the destination storage type for deliverie

Configuration

In order to be able to use Lean WM in your warehouse, you must first execute the following settings in Customizing for Shipping.

IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Map following activity as Highlighted descriptions are coming under IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Defining control parameters and number ranges for the warehouse number.

Note-We recommend that you always use warehouse number 100 as a warehouse to copy when you implement Lean WM.

Defining storage type for Lean WM.

In the SAP standard version, different examples of Lean WM storage types are preset in warehouse number 100.

Defining the picking area

In the material master, a picking area must be assigned to the material in the view Plant data / Storage 1. In the SAP standard version, two picking areas are preset.

Note-Lean WM is a prerequisite for implementation of picking areas.

Defining transfer types

In the SAP standard version, transfer type A (outbound delivery) is preset for warehouse number 100 (Lean WM). 

Defining movement types

The movement type for Lean WM supplies the information that is required for inbound and outbound deliveries.

  • Destination storage type that appears on the printout of the transfer order.
  • Control indicator for processing, confirming, and issuing transfer orders (single print or combined print).
  • Movement types 101 , 601 , and 255 are preset for Lean WM. SAP recommends keeping these movement types.

 

Defining difference indicator

the difference indicator, you can classify stock differences. This indicator is used in Lean WM for evaluation purposes only.

Defining print control

 

Synchronizing movement types between Inventory Management (MM-IM) and the Warehouse Management System (Lean WM)

Note-In the SAP standard version, all the relevant movement types are preset. SAP recommends working with the standard version.

Defining degree of activation for Warehouse Management (selection between Lean WM and full WM)

Note-Control Plant / Storage Location / Warehouse Number Assignment

 

Scalability and Flexibility

 

While lean warehouses provide sufficient flexibility for many small to medium-sized businesses, they might face limitations when scaling up to meet the demands of a rapidly growing enterprise or more complex logistical requirements

Cost Considerations

Lean warehouse solutions are more cost-effective, with lower upfront costs and reduced long-term financial commitments. This makes them an attractive option for businesses operating on tighter budgets or those seeking a quicker return on investment

Conclusion

lean warehouse solutions depend largely on the specific needs and goals of your business. if simplicity, cost-effectiveness, and quicker implementation are your priorities, a lean warehouse approach may be more suitable.

 

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/ceadbd53d34ab64ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: Re: Is Partner Profit Center available for substitution

entry.title: Selection Screen (Generic Article) buttons size
entry.content:
Hello! 

Is there a way to change the size of the Selection Screen in CCO?  I have 17 icecream flavors and I want to have more options before I scroll down.  I have tried to change the text of the article, but doesn´t changes the size of the button. Any ideas?

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Armando.

entry.title: Re: QA08 Activate despite of Stock is not creating NEW in...
entry.content: @dineshudl The configurations is correct, but i set "no inspection" in tab inspection control on QM INFO for plants A and B on purchase process and check supriment QM type configuration on material master for assurance it works.

entry.title: Re: Processing of SAP and Document Management System Inte...
entry.content:
Hi omega-ou

Could you please better describe your scenario or use case and the limitations you encountered?

There is already functionality that allows you to provide the URL to a document. 

Please check the attachments service: https://api.sap.com/api/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/overview

and https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/e7ff65b0c2c34652995745c9416edc5e.html?locale=en-US

and Manage Documents: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/70414d95dcfe4f588032ddca36f5d7ec.html?locale=en-US

You also have options on the repository you use:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/9f2f1c9e067645418a20375211e1ea04.html?locale=en-US

 

You might get a better answer if you can describe how the existing functionality or limitations are specifically not working for you today.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: SAP Disclosure Management installation on SAP HANA as...
entry.content: Thanks Marc - that is clear. Appreciate the answer. Thanks for clearing it up!

entry.title: Re: Time Sheet Workflow Based on Time Valuation Results
entry.content:
Hi @NeeleshKamath 

Thank you for very useful blog.

Is this also possible for single record approval?

If the requirement says, when employee submit overtime (attendance) request in time sheet, system evaluate the different overtime results and need to send to approver base on the result.

Can we add the business rule attach to Employee time sheet entry to check the time valuation result?

For example, on Monday employee submit overtime for 10 hours

8 hours -> OT1.5 ,need to approved by Reporting manager

2 hours -> OT2.0 ,need to approved by Reporting manager, HR

Can we also add the custom field to display OT from time valuation result in Single record approval?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ILX Curriculum Status Bubble
entry.content: @margaretstraub, as it stands, "Curriculum Compliance Ending" would be displayed when the action is required in the next 30 days. Additional details can be found here: New Learning Home Page Overview.

entry.title: Employee occupation tax is not deducted for County Montgomery city Mt. sterling
entry.content:
Hi,

I did configure Employee occupation tax (Tax type 51) for County Montgomery (KY0M) and City Mt. Sterling (KY2R). But in the payroll only City Mt. Sterling is withholding Employee occupation tax but not County Montgomery.

I checked all configuration tables and employee master data, it looks good.

Can someone tell me what could be the reason for not deducting employee occupation tax for County Montgomery.

Any help is appreciated.

Regards,

Kamala

entry.title: Re: Request for Guidance on Asset Disposal Migration in S...
entry.content:
Hi @Malla,

Could you please confirm if you're referring to deactivated assets? Please note that these are not supported.

However, you could transfer them as an active asset, and then you deactivate them by posting a complete retirement. For more detailed information:

  • Fixed asset - Master data
  • Fixed asset - Postings

You can find here the Sample Data for Data Migration.

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: BMY: Subcontracting components PO
entry.content:
Dear kyoungmo,

Can you explain the scenario. If components are self procured by subcontractor and just the finish goods are send then it can be a normal purchase.

Please share details.

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: Purchase requisitions are not visible in clip icon in Workflow.
entry.content:
I have created PR (ME51N) with the attachment file. but the attachment is not visible in Workflow

Could anyone please suggest a solution?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: What’s New in Job Monitoring for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @Nilabh_ 

Great summary, thanks!

Is it now also possible to activate monitoring of BTP Job Scheduling jobs by just easy configuration in Cloud ALM instead to develop a custom application for that?

This feature was on the Roadmap for Q4 2024.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: B1SLayer: A clean and easy way to consume SAP Business One Service Layer with .NET
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm trying to implement invoice cancellation using B1SLayer but I can't find which method to use.

I found this

POST https://localhost:50000/b1s/v1/InvoicesService_RequestApproveCancellation

{
    "Document": {
        "DocEntry": "123"
    }
}

in SAP Service Layer documentation, how can I apply it using B1SLayer?

 

Thanks in advance for any help

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3049988

entry.title: Re: Why should we consider Rule based consolidation of Investments vs Activity based in Group Report
entry.content:
Hi Naveen, many thanks for this. Is there any more documentation on how the settings 1-7 drive the consolidation of investment. For example the C/I activity flag in the settings - what are the steps the system takes for First Consolidation, and Subsequent Consolidation - for example how does the system compute what a subsequent consolidation is, and how does this affect data selection?

There doesn't seem to be much technical information on how these setting drive the consolidation, and as a consultant its hard to advise a client without this level of information. 

many thanks

Scott Farrington

entry.title: Re: Error during Down payment request creation with refer...
entry.content:
Dear Denn,

The cost element, defined as a default cost element in the Controlling Customizing, is used. This mapping is necessary for the technical check during the down payment process.

Please note that the actual posting will be made to the G/L account with the special indicator, which you have configured through "Automatic Account Determination" (ID: 100297).

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: Re: Control Restart Condition of Flexible workflow for Pu...
entry.content:
Dear Pranav,

Please check the note if it helps.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2841783

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: Re: QA08 Activate despite of Stock is not creating NEW in...
entry.content: Hi Prasath, Thank you for your feedback, and I apologize for any confusion. My concern is that some stock has already been transferred to Quality Inspection (QI) without lots due to a settings gap. I would like to move this stock to unrestricted status by creating and processing a new inspection lot using QA08, as mentioned above. If you have any alternative methods for moving this stock to unrestricted without creating a lot, please let me know. Thanks, Dinesh Kumar V

entry.title: Re: SAP DM: SFC Report does not show SFC data collected a...
entry.content:
Hi, I assume you are using a Test/Quality tenant? I experienced the same issue post 2411 release in quality a couple of weeks ago, but it´s back to normal now. That said, I suggest you check again and if the issue persist, log a ticket.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing | Manage Custom Data
entry.content:
Hello Sunny,

If the Custom Data field is in use somewhere as the error message suggests, you can´t really delete it due to the usage constraint / dependency. I suggest you try clearing up values wherever it is used and try deleting again. Otherwise, you might also try logging a ticket.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Table Function for Calendar/Date Attributes
entry.content:
hi 

need a replacement for DATE_TO_PERIOD_CONVERT FM , the above might suit the reqmt , but as Gregor suggested, is there a standard way to get the fiscal period on passing of FISCAL year variant from T009B and I_CalendarDate

Basically join of I_CalendarDate and I_FISCALPERIODRAWDATA with some manupulations.

entry.title: Re: Table Function for Calendar/Date Attributes
entry.content: @gregorw  please check and help  . need a replacment that will be acceptable in sap abap cloud

entry.title: Number range exceeded for Internal Service Confirmations
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have found that the journal entry number range was set at 7 instead of 12. Our company exceeded the number range for internal service confirmations and now has thousands of unposted source documents. Is there a possibility of changing the number range to 12 digits allowed?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Number range exceeded for Internal Service Confirmations
entry.content:
Dear All,

Once the Number Range Profile is set, it is not possible to change it later.
Refer to the KBA 3545013 - Unposted Source Documents: 'Number range interval overflow for document type XYZ' https://me.sap.com/notes/3545013 for additional details, please.

A possible solution for this issue is explained in the KBA 2565042 - Error Message Against Unposted Source Documents: Number range interval overflow for document type XYZ https://me.sap.com/notes/2565042

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Spreadsheet option missing from Download and Local file selections, but not from the Export one
entry.content:
Hello Experts Community!

This topic has been recently rised by users in the client we work with, after an Upgrade to EhP7. They say that when in trx S_ALR_87012279, going to SAP Menu--> System--> List--> Save--> Local file - the don't longer have spreadsheet option available. 

At that point we seach for related post in this forum and found that it should be standard behaviour after the upgrade to chage the naming from "Spreadsheet" to "Text with tabs". And it shoud be enough to chage the extension from .TXT to .XLS before saving, at most. 

The thing is that after doing some more tests we've found that, for example, in trx SE16N, if you go from the SAP Menu--> System--> List--> Save--> the "Spreadsheet" option is available.

So my question is if there is a way that we can unify this behaviours so the "Spreadsheet" can be accessed by clicking on "Download" and/or "Local file"?
Or is there a reasonable answer which justify this diference between options to our customer?

I appreciate any assistance you can provide us.

Best regards.
Brian

entry.title: SAP ECC Purchase Order Creation Automatic
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking for solutions, as I am kind of stuck. I am handling some SAP ECC Optimizations and the company that I work for, requires quiet a lot of approvals for everything. Do you think that it is possible to set up somehow the transformation of Purchase Requisitions into Purchase Orders, even if they require approval? I have been reading, but cannot find a way to do it. If it is possible, once we have the Purchase Requisition approved, to send a notification to user X, based on Y criteria? 

Would love to hear the opinion of an expert.


All the best,
Nikola Nikolov

entry.title: Tables and Function modules to report SRT_MONI Webservice utility Interface data for sending alert
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is to send an alert (send an email notification) to business users if the interface ended with error status (including all type of errors) available in SRT_MONI. 

The PI (CPI) response back to SAP system was successful and the webservice data has ended into an error due to incorrect data or the data doesn't exist in the SAP system (DB). For example: Company code or Sales organization doesn't exist.

Therefore, business needs notification for these error result interface data along with the Payload data /the original XML file.

To achieve this, we planned to build a report program to list out all the error resulted interface data as an output which are sent as an email notification to the business users (along with the XML file/payload data) information. 

To build the report program, we have the data stored in the table SRT_MMASTER. Using this data we need to fetch or get the Payload information and the XML file details so we can convert into xstring and send it as an attachment file. 

Please share the associated tables and the Function modules to fetch the above required data. 

If you have any other possible solution to send an alert for the above requirement, please let me know and that would be highly appreciated. 

Note:

SXMB_GET_MESSAGE_PAYLOAD  (No result when provided Message ID from the table SRT_MMASTER)

SXMB_GET_MESSAGE_LIST (No result when provided Message ID from the table SRT_MMASTER)

SRT_MONI_GET_CALL_DETAIL (codes are commented and of no use)

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Cancelled form template in S4HANA system
entry.content:
Dear khaing ,

As per my understanding this is no PO cancelled template.

You can modify the standard template or create a new one on BTP.

Regards

Sahil Grover

entry.title: Re: Modification of PO Field
entry.content: Thank you !

entry.title: The Art and Science of CAP (part 3) with Daniel Hutzel
entry.content:
Daniel Hutzel continues on his exploration of The Art and Science of CAP, sharing insights and ideas from the origins, philosophy and magic of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. #SAPCAP #TASC

Watch the previous episodes via this playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAe45xlhIfhTYB9G0mdRVjI

entry.title: QR Code button
entry.content: In the learner view, is there any way to disable/hide the QR Code button?

entry.title: HTTP 400: "External account can't create application with prefix com.sap or sap."
entry.content:
Hello, Specialist,

I am experiencing an issue when generating the App Create for an application in Mobile Service. The process fails and produces this error dump in transaction SE38, specifically in the program /MERP/CORE_CPMS_APPCREATE_PROG.

The error is not clearly logged in SMICM or other logging transactions.

After updating the /MERP/CORE and its dependencies, I was able to configure the proxy. However, this program is crashing, preventing the program from being generated.

After entering the information configured in the RFC in transaction SM59, it loads and returns this dump.

If you could help me resolve this issue, I would greatly appreciate it.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Runtime Information System user, wait inline for HTTP input
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Currently we have setup to send data from ECC to IBP cloud using HTTP.

Currently we are facing an issue with ABAP background job running too long to send the data. This is only happening in our Quality system but is working fine in the production environment. In production the background job takes 2000 seconds but in qulaity its taking more than 20,000 seconds.

When checking SM50 at application information section we get the following message: "Runtime Information: System user, wait inline for HTTP input". 

Please let me know how to resolve the issue.

Thanks

entry.title: Watch SAP Fieldglass AI and Beyond: November Release Highlights Webinar
entry.content:
We recently hosted the SAP Fieldglass AI and Beyond: November Release Highlights webinar, where we showcased the latest AI features and enhancements in the SAP Fieldglass 2024.11 release. Our SAP experts provided a comprehensive overview of the main updates, offering valuable insights into how these changes can benefit your organization.

If you missed the event or if would like to watch it again, you can access the recorded session here.

Want more information about the SAP Fieldglass 2024.11 Release?

  • Stay up to date on all SAP Fieldglass release information through the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal.
  • Join the SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone and view the Release Readiness page to access additional release resources.

entry.title: Re: HTTP 400: "External account can't create application...
entry.content:
Hello Luis,

seems to be the same problem that I just faced with the SAP Warehouse Operator application. When the test environment was created in June it was possible to create the App ID with com.sap.mobile.apps.warehouseoperator following the documentation Create a Mobile Application. But when I tried now to create the App on a new instance of SAP Mobile Service for production I got the same error. I've filed a case via SAP support and contacted @SamiLechner. Our Mobile Service instance was updated and allows now the creation of app IDs starting with com.sap. Now the onboarding of users to the Warehouse Operator App works as it seems that this SAP standard app checks the ID.

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: Future Dated Hires Provisioning from SF EC/Onboarding to Azure AD / Onprem AD
entry.content:
Hi Vslavova1,  Can you please let me know is futured hires coming from Onboarding ? If so then please check the RBP setup for SF  API user that has been used at Azure AD end. 

Hence, to ensure Azure AD/AD  to extract these future dated hires directly  from SF EC/Onboarding  specific  RBP needs to be setup  to the API user that has been created in SF EC by giving target population as All(External Onboarding User). 

If above is already in place, then pls check is user name is mapped in the Azure AD attributes configuration ?

entry.title: Re: HTTP 400: "External account can't create application...
entry.content: Okay, thank you. I will open a ticket with SAP

entry.title: Re: 1099 forms using S_P00_07000134 - SUPPRESS BACKGROUND GRAPHIC
Try before you buy: How SAP is strategically minimizing the risk of technology adoption
entry.content:
How to try SAP BTP before buying it

Trying before you buy provides a flexible way for businesses to explore SAP BTP with minimal risk. Through this program, companies—particularly existing SAP customers—can access, test, and validate solutions and tools before making a financial commitment.

Both technical and business users can participate, with direct access to a role-specific experience designed to meet their unique business requirements. This approach lets organizations experience SAP BTP in ways that support diverse business goals, enable dynamic proof-of-concept environments, and lead to the adoption of flexible consumption models.

The offering’s structure includes three tiers of engagement, each catering to different exploration and adoption needs:

  • Trial access: Users can register with an e-mail address to demo SAP BTP for up to 90 days without further obligation. This option is ideal for those seeking a hands-on introduction to cloud solutions from SAP.
  • Free tier: Users can gain ongoing, limited access to resources in SAP BTP. This model lets businesses explore tools and capabilities in real scenarios without additional costs, making it suitable for customers using SAP software for the first time.
  • Premium subscription options: SAP offers pay-as-you-go and enterprise agreement subscriptions for organizations ready to deepen their commitment. The pay-as-you-go model is consumption based, while enterprise agreements provide contract-based access that delivers up to 30% more value through consolidated usage.

What are the benefits of trying before you buy

Trying SAP BTP before you buy delivers three critical benefits that make this program an ideal choice for organizations exploring new technology investments.

  1. Low-risk experimentation

One of the top advantages of trying SAP BTP before you buy is the ability for IT teams to develop proof-of-concept models and experiment with emerging technologies with reduced financial risk. This allows for more confident decision-making and strategic planning.

The resulting flexibility allows companies to avoid the costs and potential pitfalls of large-scale implementations by confirming that the investment delivers as expected early on. Organizations can fine-tune their technology stack by testing cloud solutions in real-life scenarios to support business goals better and drive meaningful outcomes.

  1. Scalability with cost benefits

SAP’s enterprise agreement subscription provides scalability with a clear cost advantage, offering up to 30% more value than the pay-as-you-go model.

This structured model is especially beneficial for organizations aiming to expand at a predictable, lower cost, as it consolidates usage across services and resources. Businesses can scale their use of SAP BTP as needed while maintaining budget predictability and maximizing the value of their cloud investments.

  1. Proven road map for future adoption

Trying before you buy presents a strategic opportunity for our customers to deepen their engagement with the range of solutions available in SAP BTP. With flexible scaling, testing, and exploration options, the program enables customers to harness the potential of our software ecosystem without heavy up-front investments.

This approach not only enhances connectivity within the SAP software landscape but also empowers organizations to innovate confidently, knowing they can leverage a comprehensive ecosystem at their own pace. 

Where getting the most value from the cloud begins

For organizations navigating the complexities of their digital transformation, trying SAP BTP before they buy is more than a gateway to innovation. It provides a strategic framework for mitigating risk with the best-fit solutions.

SAP’s commitment to facilitating a low-risk, high-reward entry to cloud adoption is invaluable for companies looking to stay competitive in an increasingly cloud-centric world. As businesses evolve, trying before you buy serves as a practical starting point for building a comprehensive, adaptable, and cost-efficient cloud strategy.

Learn more about how you can try SAP BTP before you buy. 

Maybe is better to use “SAP customer” to sound more neutral

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content: Follow on question. In the above image the Content Modifier step 'Attempt saving' looks to be directly connected via an SFTP Receiver Adapter to the Receiver SFTP. How is this possible?

entry.title: Re: Integration Ariba with SAP ByDesign
entry.content: Hi Knut - I know this thread is probably very old. I just wanted to know where I can find details to integrate BYD - Ariba with ByD as a seller. I aware that S4/HANA-Ariba integration can be done using CIG through the Ariba Network. Has this been tried for BYD-Ariba Integration with BYD as a seller? Thanks

entry.title: Re: Change Printer in RF EWM Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi mconstanzo

As you know, the printing concept in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is different than in legacy and onprem solutions.

We now have custom field support for several Warehouse objects.

Output Parameter Determination also now allows custom fields to be used in the determination logic.

Perhaps you could define a custom field on the task or order and utilize Personas (or Adapt UI) to adapt the UI to allow picking the value for the custom field from a code list for example. (I know RF UIs can be modified with AdaptUI/Personas).

Then us that custom field in OPD to pick the output Queue? We have done something similar for printing at a customer using the "active user" 

Please investigate if any of the developer extensibility scenarios could be used.

You may also check out the extensibility explorer example of the side by side printing https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/2401

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Remove button in SU10 without using Screen Personas
entry.content:
Hi,
Our team wants to remove the button for editing single user in tcode SU01.
They say that the only way to achieve this is to install Screen Personas add-on.
However, we in the Basis team are wondering if another solution exist, to avoid having to install an new add-on just to remove 1 button in SU10.
See attachment to view the relevant button.
 
Do you know of another solution for this?
Thanks.
 



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Remove button in SU10 without using Screen Personas
entry.content:
Hello team,

an alternative to personas is to use the transaction variants.
There is an example in KBA 3242083 for SU01, but the same applies for SU10.

Best regards,
Eduardo
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: Dispatcher Reminder (BusinessProcessSteps) in Query and Business rules

entry.title: Re: Email Verification Requirement for Member Login in SA...
entry.content: Hi Kunal , Thanks for your response , Indeed my query is different , its related to a REST API. If a user is created via the `account.register` endpoint and hasn't verified their email address, I want to ensure they cannot log in. To clarify, I'm not referring to email activation through a confirmation email—this flow does not apply in my case. Instead, when the user is initially created through `account.register`, the email remains unverified. Later, an update account request will verify the email. However, in the meantime, if the user tries to log in, they should not be allowed to authenticate, nor should any token or user details be returned.

entry.title: how do I schedule a report in BO using an external scheduling document
entry.content:
Where is this documented?  I have this document:  
SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Suite
Document Version: 4.3 Support Package 4 – 2023-12-07
SAP BusinessObjects Web Intelligence User's Guide

entry.title: Re: Unpexpected Result when export CR to pdf by API Gatew...
entry.content: Still not working as I was expecting, but I noticed that creating a new Layout with NO parameters I was able to export the layout and get the base64 to save as PDF. But even a simple Layout that gets only the BusinessPartner code as DocKey fails and returns only (---)

entry.title: Re: SEN Companion Standard Content Parameters
entry.content:
Hi Emelie,

It is possible to turn on SAP Companion and display the standard content for that system, if standard content is available.  This does not require SAP Enable Now licenses.  Licenses are only required if your customer wants to display modified standard content or their own content objects.  To turn on SAP Companion to display standard content, refer to the SAP Companion Integration Guide.  The system that will contain SAP Companion must be configured for the Standard Content Only scenario.

SAP Enable Now does support Business Roles functionality to restrict access to content but given that your customer does not have licenses, there will be no ability to restrict access to the standard content.  Once SAP Companion is turned on, all users of SF will have access to that content.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Not seeing Standard Companion Content for SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
We are in the process of turning on SAP Companion for SuccessFactors.

We have gotten the ? to appear but there is no content. We've checked the Extended WA for content and found that there is some content for navigating the home page in SF but that is not appearing. We also cannot get into edit. 

How do we get the standard content for SF to appear?

How do we get into the edit mode to create content?

entry.title: Re: S4HC Supplier Operational Evaluation Score Calculation Examples
entry.content:
Hi Qi,

Great information. How the score is calculated for PPM is still a mystery for me and my client. We understand the number of PPM calculated but is the final score based on the weighting and number of the count of the returns(and/or qm notification)? The result of test I have run is not consistent.

entry.title: Re: line-by-line tax issue while invoice posting
entry.content:
ls_tax_accnt-itemno_tax = 000001 and ls_accntgl-itemno_tax = 000001.

Suppose we have multiple line items in one document, so shall we keep 000001 - 000002 - 000003 in sequential manner or against each of the line it should be hardcoded as 000001 only in Itemno_tax field ?

entry.title: Re: Error activating Business Content Sales Analysis in S...
entry.content:
Dear @RaulGil ,

We have released a new version 1.3.1 of the package Sales Analysis for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Could you please re-try installing this version? It should resolve the issue observed.
Apologizes for any inconvenience this may have caused.

Best regards
Guido

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 and OData UploadCollection Example
entry.content:
Nice blog.

Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is a concept.
"Premises" is a location.

"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: Re: Migrating from CR9 nowadays

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content: Hi Bryan, that's not Content Modifier, that's 'Send' component, they share the same icon

entry.title: Re: Team View (second manager) on compensation worksheet
entry.content:
Hi @buddepu ,

For the custom/second manager hierarchy to work, you must populate this value for all users in the hierarchy.  If there are any circular references or a break in the hierarchy structure, this filter will not work as you expect.  Please see this KBA on creating a custom hierarchy.  Note that the top of the hierarchy must have a blank value in their SECOND_MANAGER field and not NO_MANAGER.

Hope this helps.

Christian

entry.title: Re: Extend Datasphere Business content package.
entry.content:
Hi @RaulGil ,

since some time SAP Datasphere offers the so called "Space Mapping" option during content import (find more details here under 4/Content).
This option helps you to easier extend the content:

  • Import the content at least twice: once into the "standard" SAP_CONTENT space and once in another Space (YOUR Space) that you planned to use to extend the content and use the content productively
  • With this setup you can always import any update of a content package into the SAP_CONTENT space w/o harming any productively used and extend content.
  • Decide on-demand if and which update you might want to consume in YOUR space.
  • The Space Mapping option is also available for any package in the Content Network - to support flexible transport scenarios.

HTH
Best regards
Guido

entry.title: Signavio Scenario Modelling
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How can I model scenarios in Signavio while keeping perspective of where the model is linked in to the bigger picture? I'm thinking of something similar to the Solution Value Flows you see in the Process Navigator but modelled within Signavio?

Ideally, I want the ability to maximize and minimize different process levels until I've drilled down to our bottom layer (Level 7 in this instance).

entry.title: Re: Import of Package Sales Analysis failed - Status Obje...
entry.content:
Hi @Maike151 ,

The error message is somehow unspecific. All other objects but the Analytic Models have already been imported in previous imports, correct?
There may have been a temporary issue while importing the content package, thus I would recommend to import the package again - latest version is 1.3.1.

In general you may always install the package entirely and then create the Replication Flows. But a "staged" approach to first install everything below should also work.

Best regards,
Guido

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from a Custom SAP Fiori Application
entry.content:
Hi Manisha,

After creating a destination in BTP for the SPA api.

2. Configure the destination in ui5.yaml file 

3. configure the destination in xs-app.json file

4. Build and deploy to BTP.

Now, you can check SAP Build Process -> Monitoring -> Process and Workflow Instances

 

Thanks,

Santosh

entry.title: Re: Business Content Import Error in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @Rufus_Samuel ,

an internal server error often points to some temporary issue. While this should obviously not happen, please such case always give the import a second try.
If this does not work, check if the object mentioned in the error message might already exist in the Space and delete and the re-run the import.
I hope you finally succeeded to import the content package.

Best regards
Guido

entry.title: Maintain classification in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts

We would like to implement S/4HANA International Trade. What are the options to mass create control classes (ECCN)? From the SAP best practices, there seem to be a direct integration with a data provider. But would it be possible to load a file (XML or CSV) without setting up an integration with data providers?

 

Thanks in advance,

Stéphanie

entry.title: Re: Not seeing Standard Companion Content for SAP Success...

Hi Susan,

A good place to start with correctly setting up SAP Companion is the Getting Started with SAP Companion Community Post.  This provides an overview of all of the key tasks that must be performed to setup SAP Companion correctly.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Issue creating Template in SAP Cloud Appliance
entry.content:
Hello I followed instructions in this video : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hteLyswrhsE but when I go to the SAP cloud appliance I got error: 

The subscription policy limit for resource type 'Total Regional vCPUs' was exceeded. The limit for resource type 'Total Regional vCPUs' is 4 per subscription, the current count is 0, and the requested increment is 44. The subscription policy limit for resource type 'Standard ESv5 Family vCPUs' was exceeded. The limit for resource type 'Standard ESv5 Family vCPUs' is 4 per subscription, the current count is 0, and the requested increment is 40. Please contact Microsoft Azure support to enable the required resources.

Do I need to realise something previously in MS Azure for example?

- To create thr resource for the machines

- To assign some role?

- Is the video completed? 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Not seeing Standard Companion Content for SAP Success...
entry.content: Thanks, Shane. We will be looking at those permissions for admins, and reading through. In the meantime, I was able to get into edit mode and create a few simple help tiles and my users are going to appreciate that! Can you please confirm that we should be able to see the standard content for SF where it exists in the Connected WA?

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture and how to get started
entry.content: Insightful article for someone that is very much on the business side like me. The way you explain the importance of aligning business strategies with IT through Enterprise Architecture frameworks makes a lot of sense, and was an eye opener for me.

entry.title: Re: Not seeing Standard Companion Content for SAP Success...
entry.content:
Hi Susan,

Yes, you should be able to see standard Simulations in the Learning tab of SAP Companion.  There are no standard Guided Tours, Message Bubbles or Link Tiles for SF.  If it is only the standard Simulations that are an issue, try publishing one of the Simulations and see if it appears in the Learning tab.  If it does, I suspect that publishing is the issue, however, the standard content should already be published.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about issues stopping replication flow.
entry.content: I am experiencing the same problem. RF just sits there with "Stopping" status and nothing happen. Can't restart, can't pause or anything after that. The only option I had (and by no means is a solution) was to delete and recreate the RF. I couldn't wait as we were on deadline project. I have open SAP ticket to see what they say. Will post here if I find solution.

entry.title: Re: SFTP adapter - how to handle errors within processing a file
entry.content: Ah! I had to go back and look at the icon. That explains it. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Enable Simulation for Flexible Workflow Scenarios in Manage Workflows
entry.content:
Hi @SBach,

The blog is quite insightful, thank you. In S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD 2408, could you please clarify where we can maintain value helps for different workflow scenarios as there is no option for it in Fiori apps - 'Manage Workflow Scenarios' or 'Manage Workflows for...'.

Thank you in advance.

Kind Regards,

Praneetha

entry.title: icm http log and web dispatcher output with delimiter
entry.content:
Hi

Our icm/HTTP/logging logformat is 

 LOGFORMAT=%t %а %u "%r" %s %b %Lms %{Host}i %P %w1 %w2%y1 %у2 %h %h0 %h1 


When exporting the logs from the Web Dispatcher, the txt file extracts are not easily analysed as there's hundreds of thousands of logs records with various log structures which do not parse into consistent columns for review (I'm looking at approx 400k log record lines).

Is it possible to introduce a delimiter to the logformat, like pipe |, to separate the parameter outputs ?  

or is there a better alternative to view the specific http logs ?  
I need to identify IP addresses for all "trusted" log entries.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: 🌟 Bem-vindos à nossa Comunidade SAP em Curitiba!
entry.content:
Estou muito feliz em poder participar desta comunidade SAP.

Meu nome é Jeferson Machado Da Silva, sou um gaucho que venho para o Parana estudar e acabou gostando tanto daqui que ficou, resido em uma pequena cidade chamada Palotina.

Trabalho atualmente em uma grande Cooperativa que atua em diversos segmentos, a cinco anos atuo de alguma forma diretamente com os produtos da SAP. Hoje sou Analista de Segurança da Informação com foco na gestão de acessos e identidades SAP.

Espero poder participar de algum evento da comunidade presencial para crescer mais meu networking e poder colaborar com meu conhecimento.

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay - Assistance Needed with Individual Goal...
entry.content:
Hi @JoannaRo 

I need to check but I believe that the field that needs to be used with ABR is performancePercent instead of individualPercent.

Can you share screenshots of how it looks so far ? 

Are you pulling the Ratings from each performance form correctly for each time ? I am surprised because last time I tried a few months ago, Overlap was not giving me the expected results and I had to use startDate and a setting in provisioning to enforce it on the first record to make it work correctly.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to increase the length of the WBS Desc...
entry.content:
The best practice for your requirement is to create customer defined attributes, and in S/4HANA this is much easier than it used to be.  It's called In-App Extensibility and allows you to create as many additional attributes as you need.  These additional fields can be seen in standard reporting.

Also, in standard SAP there is the long text option next to the WBS Description.   With long text the sky is the limit for adding as much text as you need, but it doesn't show up in standard reporting.

entry.title: MIGO - Goods issue does not recognize Fund Center
entry.content:
Dear colleagues

I am having trouble generating a goods issue in transaction MIGO because, for a specific movement type, the Fund Center is entered manually, but the system ignores it and derives a different one from the FMDERIVE rules. I ran the process with the trace active and noticed that the Fund Center field is coming through as empty, ignoring the one that was entered manually.

Any suggestions on how to address this?

 

 

Thank you in advance for your help!

Kind Regards

Humberto F

entry.title: Re: Jump to ECC from SAC
entry.content:
Hello Joslee,

I have a similar issue. We have created a SAC story on the source "2CCFIMSPLANACT2501" to show our margin analysis statement.

Now the colleagues want to see the details with the line item report on G/L account level. 

The idea was to create a second linked story in which we see the details. But I am not able to create a line item report as a story. The line item report I found are always provided via ODATA-Service and until now I didn't find a way to create a story with a ODATA-Service.

What I already managed is to integrate the Fiori App (Line Item Report) via Web Page into the story - but here the look and feel is different as well as the parameters aren't taken over?

So it would be great if someone has an idea how to set-up a line item report in the SAC environment as story.

Have a nice day,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Adding Attachments to Emails in SAP CPI Using Groovy

3284639 - Changes to Groovy Scripts: Upgrading to the Attachment Wrapper - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Supplier Batch CDS Views for S/4 Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi @mbha, This field is not releaded in these cds view now. Would you please consider to create a custom field and also enable it the related CDS view to show it.

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads
entry.content:
@TimoMaier 

Thank you for your reply.

I have updated my test class as below, but the events are still not being triggered. (Outbound topic binding has already been configured)

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    DATA lt_event TYPE zbp_i_parkeddocheader=>tt_events.

    cl_abap_tx=>save( ).

    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zi_parkeddocheader
      WHERE sourcecompanycode = '1510'
        AND companycode = '1510'
        AND sourcefiscalyear = '2024'
        AND sourceaccountingdocument = '0100000052'
        INTO @DATA(doc).

    lt_event = VALUE #( ( %key = VALUE #( sourcecompanycode = doc-sourcecompanycode
                                          companycode = doc-companycode
                                          sourcefiscalyear = doc-sourcefiscalyear
                                          sourceaccountingdocument = doc-sourceaccountingdocument )
                           %param = CORRESPONDING #( doc )
                       ) ).
    zbp_i_parkeddocheader=>raise_parkeddoccreated( lt_event ).

    out->write( 'Event triggerd' ).

  ENDMETHOD.

As an alternative approach, I have defined an action which triggers emission of an event.

managed implementation in class zbp_i_parkeddocheader unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZI_ParkedDocHeader
with unmanaged save
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
{
  update;

  event ParkedDocCreated parameter ZA_ParkedDocumentCreated;
  action raiseParkedDocumentCreated;
}

 

The test code for triggering an event is below. This worked and the event was visible in the event monitor.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_ParkedDocHeader
      ENTITY ZI_ParkedDocHeader
      EXECUTE raiseParkedDocumentCreated
      from value #( ( %key = value #( SourceCompanyCode = '1510'
                                      CompanyCode = '1510'
                                      SourceFiscalYear = '2024'
                                      SourceAccountingDocument = '0100000052' ) ) ).

    COMMIT ENTITIES.

    out->write( 'Event triggerd' ).

  ENDMETHOD.
